import cv2
from PIL import Image, ImageOps

def preprocess_image(image_path, target_size=(256, 256), grayscale=False, crop_center=True, resize=True):
    """Preprocess an image for computer vision tasks using OpenCV and Pillow."""
    # Load image
    image = cv2.imread(image_path)
    if image is None:
        raise ValueError(f"Image at {image_path} could not be loaded.")

    # Convert to PIL Image for easier manipulation
    pil_image = Image.open(image_path)
    if grayscale:
        pil_image = pil_image.convert("L")

    # Resize image
    if resize:
        pil_image = pil_image.resize(target_size, Image.ANTIALIAS)

    # Crop center if required
    if crop_center:
        width, height = pil_image.size
        left = (width - target_size[0]) // 2
        top = (height - target_size[1]) // 2
        pil_image = pil_image.crop((left, top, left + target_size[0], top + target_size[1]))

    # Convert back to OpenCV format
    processed_image = cv2.cvtColor(pil_image, cv2.COLOR_RGB2BGR)

    return processed_image

# Example usage
if __name__ == "__main__":
    image_path = "input_image.jpg"
    preprocessed_img = preprocess_image(image_path)
    cv2.imwrite("preprocessed_image.jpg", preprocessed_img)
